scenario: A new GitHub visitor, contributor, or coding agent can understand the product, install it, find the right contribution path, file useful public-safe issues, and open reviewable PRs without reading private sprint history.
expected_public_safe_evidence: README links setup, contributing, agent instructions, security, code of conduct, privacy, and claim audit
CONTRIBUTING explains issue routing, local setup, validation, evidence, agent-authored PRs, review threads, and safety boundaries
AGENTS includes a repository-agent first-read checklist
structured issue forms require repro, impact, safety, and redaction fields
PR template asks for problem, user impact, validation, safety boundary, evidence, and agent-authored disclosure
package files include public contributor and agent docs
scorecard and tests fail if the community funnel regresses
pass_criteria: README gives a first-screen product promise, setup link, contribution link, agent link, and safety boundary without fake popularity claims.
CONTRIBUTING gives humans and agents a clear path for setup, issue routing, PR validation, evidence, review-thread handling, and safe contribution scope.
AGENTS gives coding agents a concise first-read checklist before implementation.
GitHub issue templates are structured forms for bug, docs bug, feature, adapter, protocol drift, and unsafe-control reports.
Issue and PR templates require public-safe evidence and redaction checks.
CODE_OF_CONDUCT and SECURITY exist and route conduct versus vulnerability reports separately.
The package includes README, CONTRIBUTING, AGENTS, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, SECURITY, LICENSE, docs, skills, and evals.

The public community funnel does not overclaim unsupported adapter parity, hosted synchronization, unattended control, permission bypass, generic GUI mutation, enterprise/customer readiness, or fake stars.
fail_criteria: README buries setup or contribution paths behind internal sprint history.
Contributors cannot tell which issue form to use.
Agent-authored PRs lack instructions for issue linkage, validation, evidence, or review-thread cleanup.
Public issue templates invite raw transcripts, tokens, credentials, private DBs, screenshots with private data, or customer data.
Repo docs claim star growth, trending status, unsupported adapter parity, hosted synchronization, unattended control, generic GUI mutation, or enterprise/customer readiness without proof.
known_gaps: This scorecard proves repository-readiness surfaces only; it does not prove actual star growth, GitHub Trending placement, social distribution, support capacity, stable 1.0 readiness, npm latest promotion, or customer readiness.
Repository metadata such as GitHub description and topics still require owner/admin update outside the file diff when wording changes.
next_action: Run the public-community-funnel test and scorecard sweep before claiming GA docs or contributor readiness.
proof_boundary: This proves public-safe GitHub community-funnel readiness for docs, issue forms, PR templates, and agent contribution routing only; it does not prove star growth, GitHub Trending placement, social reach, stable release, npm publish, live Codex control, GUI mutation, adapter parity, or customer readiness.
